skill.name: vercel-deploy
skill.description: Deploy any project to Vercel instantly. Auto-detects frameworks, creates deployment packages, and returns live preview URLs with claimable deployment links.
skill.summary: Instant Vercel deployments with automatic framework detection and zero authentication required.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 36 external command patterns and 8 network references. All findings are false positives: shell commands use hardcoded arguments or safely handled variables for legitimate deployment operations. Network access targets a single hardcoded Vercel endpoint. Temp file creation uses secure mktemp with proper cleanup. No command injection, credential theft, or data exfiltration risks detected.

low_findings.title: External Network Request
low_findings.description: Script makes POST request to external deployment endpoint. This is required for core functionality but users should be aware their code is uploaded to a third-party service.

low_findings.title: User Path Handling
low_findings.description: Script accepts user-provided directory paths as arguments. While tar excludes node_modules and .git, users should verify which files are being uploaded.

content.value_statement: Deploy any project to Vercel without authentication or configuration. The skill packages your code, detects your framework automatically, and returns live preview URLs in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Package project directories into deployment tarballs (excludes node_modules and .git)
Auto-detect 40+ frameworks from package.json including Next.js, React, Vue, Svelte, Astro
Upload deployments to Vercel via secure API endpoint
Return live preview URL for immediate site access
Provide claim URL to transfer deployment to user's Vercel account
Handle static HTML projects by renaming single HTML files to index.html
limitations: Cannot deploy to user's personal Vercel account without using the claim URL
Requires network access to claude-skills-deploy.vercel.com endpoint
Framework detection relies on package.json dependencies only
Cannot customize deployment settings or environment variables

use_cases.title: Quick prototype hosting
use_cases.description: Deploy a working prototype or demo for client review without setting up accounts or CI/CD pipelines.

use_cases.title: Portfolio site deployment
use_cases.description: Instantly publish personal portfolio sites, project demos, or landing pages with shareable URLs.

use_cases.title: Preview builds for testing
use_cases.description: Create temporary preview deployments for testing responsive designs or sharing work-in-progress with teams.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy current project
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my current project to Vercel and give me the preview link.
prompt_templates.scenario: You have a completed project in your working directory ready to deploy.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy specific directory
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy the project at /path/to/my-site to Vercel.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want to deploy a project located in a specific directory.

prompt_templates.title: Deploy and share URLs
prompt_templates.prompt: Deploy my website and provide both the preview URL and the claim URL so I can transfer it to my account later.
prompt_templates.scenario: You want immediate deployment with option to claim ownership in your Vercel account.

prompt_templates.title: Troubleshoot deployment failure
prompt_templates.prompt: My Vercel deployment failed. Check if it's a network restriction issue and guide me through enabling the required domains.
prompt_templates.scenario: Deployment failed due to network restrictions on claude.ai.

best_practices: Review your project directory before deploying to ensure no sensitive files are included
Save both the preview URL and claim URL for future reference
Claim your deployment in your Vercel account for long-term projects to maintain ownership
anti_patterns: Do not deploy projects containing API keys, credentials, or sensitive configuration files
Do not rely on the skill deployment for production-critical applications without claiming ownership
Do not attempt to deploy very large projects as they may exceed upload limits

faq.question: Do I need a Vercel account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No authentication is required. The skill deploys to a shared endpoint and returns a preview URL. Use the claim URL to transfer the deployment to your personal Vercel account if desired.

faq.question: What files are included in the deployment?
faq.answer: All files in the specified directory are included except node_modules and .git folders. These exclusions keep deployment packages small and exclude unnecessary files.

faq.question: Which frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill auto-detects 40+ frameworks including Next.js, Gatsby, Remix, Astro, Nuxt, SvelteKit, Vue, React, Angular, and many more by analyzing your package.json dependencies.

faq.question: What happens if deployment fails?
faq.answer: Common failures are due to network restrictions. On claude.ai, add *.vercel.com to allowed domains in settings. The skill will display specific error messages to help troubleshoot.

faq.question: Can I deploy static HTML without a package.json?
faq.answer: Yes. For static HTML projects, the framework is set to null. If you have a single HTML file that is not named index.html, it will be automatically renamed.

faq.question: How long do preview deployments last?
faq.answer: Preview deployments remain active as long as they receive traffic. For permanent hosting, claim the deployment in your Vercel account to maintain full control.
